name: Testim AI Test Automation API
what_it_does: AI-powered test automation platform with REST API — enabling QA and dev teams to record, maintain, and execute end-to-end web tests using ML for smart element identification, automatic test maintenance when UI changes, parallel test execution, and CI/CD integration.
use_cases: Triggering test suite execution via API for CI/CD pipeline integration
Querying test run results and failure reports for build status dashboards
Managing test suites and groupings programmatically for test organization
Pulling test execution analytics for QA performance and reliability reporting
Automating test assignment to parallel execution grids via API
not_for: API testing and load testing (use Postman or k6 for those)
Unit testing and component testing (use Jest or Vitest)
Mobile app testing without web component (use Appium or Detox)
best_when: A QA or engineering team needs reliable, self-healing end-to-end web tests with ML-based element matching that reduces test maintenance burden when UI changes.
avoid_when: You primarily need API-level testing, unit testing, or mobile app testing without web component.

auth.notes: API token from Testim account. Bearer token auth. Project-scoped access (project ID required in requests). No OAuth2. Webhook for test completion events. CLI runner available for local and CI execution. Acquired by Tricentis in 2022.

pricing.notes: Israeli company (Tel Aviv). Founded 2014. Acquired by Tricentis in 2022. ML-based element matching reduces flaky test maintenance. Self-healing tests automatically adapt to UI changes. Parallel execution on Testim grid or custom Selenium/WebDriver grid. CI integrations: GitHub Actions, Jenkins, CircleCI. Strong enterprise QA adoption.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for test runs, suites, results, and labels. Bearer token auth. Project ID required in most requests. Good documentation via help center. Webhook for run completion. CLI runner for local/CI execution. Tricentis acquisition may affect API roadmap — verify current docs.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Test run reads idempotent by run ID. Suite reads idempotent. Result reads idempotent. New test run trigger creates new run — not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Project ID required in all API requests — different from account ID
Tests must be recorded via Testim Editor (Chrome extension) before API execution
Tricentis acquisition may affect pricing and API — verify current terms
ML element matching requires initial learning period for new test environments
Webhook delivery may retry — implement idempotent webhook handlers

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. US data residency. Bearer token auth. Test execution on Testim cloud — application under test credentials must be secured in test config. Tricentis enterprise security standards post-acquisition.
